subject: Statistics
title: Fisher and the Lady Tasting Tea
description: Learn Fisher and the Lady Tasting Tea in one minute.

hook.text: A lady claims she can taste whether milk was added before or after tea. Fisher designed an experiment to test her.

buildup.text: Fisher presented 8 cups — 4 milk-first, 4 tea-first — in random order. She had to identify all 8 correctly.

discovery.text: The probability of getting all 8 right by chance is 1 in 70. If she succeeded, the evidence was strong.

twist.text: This simple experiment led Fisher to develop the exact test and the foundations of experimental design.

climax.text: She reportedly got them all right. A tea party moment created the framework for modern statistical testing.

punchline.text: A tea party launched a revolution. Design the test right.

quiz.question: What statistical concept did Fisher develop from the lady tasting tea experiment?
options: The exact test and foundational principles of experimental design
